content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Anton and Polyhedrons". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/problemset/problem/785/A
constraints: The name of each polyhedron is one of the following: Tetrahedron, Cube, Octahedron, Dodecahedron, Icosahedron
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer n, which is the number of polyhedrons. Then n lines follow, each containing the name of a polyhedron.
content.outputFormat: Print the number of tetrahedrons, cubes, octahedrons, dodecahedrons and icosahedrons.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed
general mixed case
hidden.input:
7
Tetrahedron
Cube
Octahedron
Dodecahedron
Icosahedron
Tetrahedron
Cube

solution.approach: Simple counting based on the given polyhedron names

code.Python3:
n = int(input())
tet = cube = octa = dode = icosa = 0
for _ in range(n):
    s = input()
    if s == 'Tetrahedron': tet += 1
    elif s == 'Cube': cube += 1
    elif s == 'Octahedron': octa += 1
    elif s == 'Dodecahedron': dode += 1
    elif s == 'Icosahedron': icosa += 1
print(tet, cube, octa, dode, icosa)
